content: Non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), now increasingly termed metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD), encompasses a spectrum from simple hepatic steatosis to non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH/MASH) to cirrhosis and hepatocellular carcinoma. Definition: hepatic fat accumulation >5% of hepatocytes in absence of significant alcohol consumption (<21 drinks/week men, <14 women) and secondary causes. Pathophysiology: 'multiple-hit' hypothesis involving insulin resistance, lipotoxicity, oxidative stress, inflammation, gut microbiome alterations. Strongly associated with metabolic syndrome components (obesity, T2DM, dyslipidemia, hypertension). Prevalence ~25% globally, higher in Hispanics, lower in African Americans. NASH (steatosis + inflammation + hepatocyte injury) affects 20-30% of NAFLD patients; 10-20% of NASH progresses to cirrhosis over decades. Leading indication for liver transplantation in US.
